questions.question: Describe the main difference between an extensive game with perfect information and an extensive game with imperfect information. Provide an example to support your answer. (15 marks)
A player in an extensive game with perfect information can see all the moves made by their opponent. What is the strategic implication of this for this player? (10 marks)
Suppose Alice and Bob play a game of chess that has no end condition defined. Given perfect information, what is the optimal strategy for Alice against Bob? Explain your reasoning. (20 marks)
